# control-up: verify that af control up/down/status drive superterm and
# tailscale serve correctly.
#
# Uses the fake superterm and tailscale binaries provided by writeExternalFakes
# in testscript_test.go. Both fakes are stateless: superterm up always prints
# a local URL; tailscale serve --bg always prints a tailnet URL.
#
# Two scenarios are exercised:
#   1. happy path   — af control up prints both URLs; status shows live; down succeeds.
#   2. missing tool — override PATH to hide superterm; af control up must fail.

# ── Scenario 1: happy path ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

exec af control up
stdout 'http://localhost:7681'
stdout 'https://fake-node.fake-tailnet.ts.net'
stdout 'Remote control active'

exec af control status
stdout 'http://localhost:7681'
stdout 'https://fake-node.fake-tailnet.ts.net'

exec af control down
stdout 'remote control stopped'

# ── Scenario 2: unsupported provider ─────────────────────────────────────────
# af control up --provider unknown must return an error referencing "superterm".

! exec af control up --provider unknown
stderr 'unsupported'

# ── Scenario 3: missing superterm ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Place a broken-superterm wrapper (exits 127) in a new bin directory prepended
# ahead of the normal fakebin. The tailscale fake remains reachable from fakebin.
# af control up must fail and mention superterm.

mkdir $WORK/badsuperterm
exec sh -c 'printf "#!/bin/sh\nexit 127\n" > $WORK/badsuperterm/superterm && chmod 755 $WORK/badsuperterm/superterm'
env PATH=$WORK/badsuperterm:$PATH

! exec af control up
stderr 'superterm'
